title: Fix persistent profile loading / infinite spinner blocking dashboard access

requirements.summary: Create a `useActorReady.ts` wrapper hook that derives `isActorReady` from the existing `useActor` hook, ensuring it returns `true` only when both the Internet Identity principal is fully resolved and non-anonymous, and the actor instance is confirmed non-null
acceptanceCriteria: `isActorReady` is `false` while the identity is initializing or the actor is null.
`isActorReady` becomes `true` only when the actor is non-null and the principal is authenticated and resolved.
No existing immutable file is modified.

file_operations.description: Create a new hook that wraps `useActor` and `useInternetIdentity` to derive and export a stable `isActorReady` boolean. The hook returns `true` only when: (1) the Internet Identity client has finished initializing (`isInitializing === false`), (2) the user is authenticated (identity is non-null and principal is not anonymous), and (3) the actor instance from `useActor` is non-null. Export the `isActorReady` flag along with the actor instance for consumer convenience.

requirements.summary: Rewrite `useQueries.ts` to gate all query hooks (especially `useGetProfile`) on `isActorReady`, include the authenticated principal in cache keys, and treat `null` backend responses as 'profile not found' rather than errors
acceptanceCriteria: Cache key includes the principal so re-authentication triggers a fresh fetch.
A `null` backend response sets a `profileNotFound` flag / returns `data: null` without entering the error state.
A thrown error sets `isError: true` and surfaces a retry mechanism.

file_operations.description: Refactor all query hooks to import and use `isActorReady` from the new `useActorReady` hook. For `useGetProfile` (which calls `getCallerUserProfile`), change the `enabled` flag to strictly gate on `isActorReady === true`, and update the cache key to `['profile', principalText]` (derive `principalText` from the identity hook). Modify the `queryFn` so that a `null` return value from the backend is treated as valid data (profile not found / new user) and does not trigger the error state; only thrown exceptions should set `isError: true`. Apply the same `isActorReady` gating to all other query hooks (custom links, vault notes, etc.).

requirements.summary: Rewrite `ProtectedRoute.tsx` to implement a strict state machine that prevents premature redirects, shows a full-page loading skeleton while `isActorReady` or profile query is loading, renders an error state with a Retry button on fetch failure, redirects to `/login` only when definitively unauthenticated, shows `ProfileSetupModal` for new users, and renders children when profile data is present
acceptanceCriteria: No redirect to `/login` occurs while identity or actor is still initializing.
A full-page spinner is visible until both actor and profile query resolve.
A 'Retry' button is shown when the profile fetch errors.
New users see `ProfileSetupModal` instead of a redirect.
Authenticated users with a profile see the dashboard without an infinite spinner.

file_operations.description: Ensure `useGetProfile` exports both the standard React Query return values (`data`, `isLoading`, `isError`, `refetch`) and a derived `profileNotFound` boolean (true when `data === null` and `isError === false`). This flag will be consumed by `ProtectedRoute` to distinguish 'new user' from 'error' states.

Rewrite the component to import `isActorReady` from `useActorReady` and `useGetProfile` from `useQueries`. Implement the following state machine: (1) If `isActorReady === false` OR profile query `isLoading === true`, render a full-page military-themed loading skeleton (gold spinner on matte black background, no redirect). (2) If profile query `isError === true`, render a full-page error state with a gold-accented 'Retry' button that calls `refetch()`. (3) If the user is definitively unauthenticated (identity hook confirms not logged in AND `isActorReady === true`), redirect to `/login`. (4) If `profileNotFound === true` (data is null but no error), render `<ProfileSetupModal />` without redirecting. (5) If profile data is present, render `<Outlet />` or children. This sequencing eliminates race conditions and prevents infinite spinners.

requirements.summary: Audit `App.tsx` `AuthGuard` component to ensure it waits for Internet Identity initialization to complete before making any routing decision, rendering the same full-page loading skeleton used in `ProtectedRoute` while `isInitializing` is `true`
acceptanceCriteria: `AuthGuard` never redirects while the identity client is still initializing.
A loading skeleton is shown during initialization.
After initialization, routing decisions (redirect vs. render children) are correct.

file_operations.description: Refactor the `AuthGuard` component to check the `isInitializing` flag from `useInternetIdentity`. While `isInitializing === true`, render the same full-page military-themed loading skeleton used in `ProtectedRoute` (gold spinner on matte black background). Once initialization completes (`isInitializing === false`), proceed with the existing routing logic: if unauthenticated, redirect to `/login`; otherwise render children. This prevents premature redirects during the identity client's startup phase.
